div.h1: ABA Problem Timeline: CAS Pointer Race Condition
div.h3: Memory Recycling & Pointer Identity Vulnerability Analysis
div.p: Engineering Specification: Demonstrates a failure in lock-free synchronization where a CAS (Compare-and-Swap) succeeds based on pointer value identity, ignoring that the underlying memory node has been deallocated and repurposed.

ul.li: Pointer Identity Overload: In Step 8, the hardware Compare-and-Swap instruction performs a bitwise comparison. It succeeds because the pointer value 0x00A is identical to the snapshot, despite the semantic data at that address being completely different (Node A vs Node A').
Use-After-Free (UAF): The stack HEAD is successfully updated to 0x00B. However, 0x00B was returned to the allocator's free list in Step 5.

ol.li: Segfault: Next pop() attempts to read 0x00B->next, accessing unmapped or protected memory.
Silent Corruption: 0x00B might be re-allocated for a different object type, leading to arbitrary memory writes.
Tagged State (Double-Width CAS): Use cmpxchg16b. Pair the pointer with a 64-bit generation counter. Even if the pointer is reused, the counter mismatch causes CAS failure.
Hazard Pointers: Active threads register the pointers they are accessing. The free() operation is deferred until no hazard pointers reference the target address.
Epoch-Based Reclamation (EBR): Memory is retired into a 'limbo' state and only physically freed once all threads have exited the current protective epoch.

g.text: PHASE 3: False CAS Success & Heap Corruption
